hypotheses.statement: Paper second-chance displacement re-eval admits some previously displacement_blocked intents without weakening first-pass displacement authority.
Displacement_blocked rows show positive mean Variant-A 60m counterfactual expectancy in the blocked-why corpus.
Under documented ATR stop/TP/time-stop proxy, a majority of emulator grid cells show positive mean USD PnL on displacement_blocked-covered rows.
Decision-time features weakly separate GOOD vs BAD displacement_blocked outcomes at 60m (univariate rules).

paper_metrics.conclusion_AB: A) We can separate GOOD vs BAD displacement blocks with decision-time features (see top rules).
